title: mfa — HTTP routes
description: Tenant-facing MFA self-service surface + platform-admin recovery + support endpoints. Every tenant-audience route is under /api/v1/me/mfa; every platform-admin route is under /api/v1/platform/mfa. Login-time MFA challenge issuance / verification lives in identity/auth (POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/verify) — this module owns the STEADY-STATE self-service surface (list, enroll, manage, regenerate) plus the mid-session step-up verify (POST /api/v1/me/mfa/challenge/{id}/verify) that gates sensitive actions after the initial login.

routes.description: Returns MfaMethodsSummary DTO — which methods enrolled, recovery-codes remaining, step-up requirements.
Round 1. Generates a fresh TOTP secret (base32 + otpauth:// URI for QR rendering), holds in Redis for 5 minutes. Emits MfaEnrollmentPreviewIssued.

Round 2. Consumes the preview + confirms the TOTP code proves possession, persists ciphertext to Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted, generates recovery codes. Emits MfaEnrollmentConfirmed + identity::MfaEnrolled. Response includes the RecoveryCodesDownload DTO (plaintext codes shown ONCE).
Disable TOTP factor. Requires a fresh MFA challenge (recovery code OR WebAuthn OR TOTP itself). Emits MfaDisabled if this was the last MFA factor.
Round 1. Returns WebauthnRegisterOptions DTO for navigator.credentials.create(). Holds the challenge in Redis for 5 minutes.
Round 2. Verifies the attestation response, inserts WebauthnCredential row. Emits WebauthnCredentialRegistered.
List caller's registered credentials. filter[include_disabled] toggles disabled_at IS NULL.

Rename or disable a credential. Body: { device_name?, disabled_at? }. disabled_at: 'now' | 'null' (setting null on a disabled credential is refused by the observer). Emits WebauthnCredentialRenamed OR WebauthnCredentialDisabled.
Hard-delete a credential immediately (bypasses the 90-day retention purge). Emits WebauthnCredentialDeleted(trigger=user_action). If this was the caller's last MFA factor, emits MfaDisabled.
Discard the current recovery-code set + generate a fresh set. Emits MfaRecoveryCodesRegenerated + identity::RecoveryCodesRegenerated. Response includes RecoveryCodesDownload DTO.

Emit the CURRENT unconsumed recovery codes ONE MORE TIME. Audit-logged as data-egress. Response includes RecoveryCodesDownload DTO. Different from regenerate — download does NOT invalidate the current codes.

groups.name: tenant-mfa-challenge
groups.description: Mid-session step-up challenge verification. Note: challenge INITIATION lives on identity/auth (POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/challenge/initiate); this module owns only the VERIFY step.

routes.description: Submit a code / assertion / recovery code for a challenge. Consumes the challenge atomically on success; sets session's last_mfa_challenge_at + mfa_challenge_completed_at. Body: VerifyChallengeInput. Response: success + updated MfaMethodsSummary. Fires MfaChallengeSucceeded OR MfaChallengeFailed.

groups.name: platform-mfa
groups.description: Platform-admin support + recovery surface. Every write is audit-critical.

routes.description: Show MFA posture for one User (across every Application via the shared Identity). Redacted response — no plaintext, no bytea, no credential_id_b64url.
Emergency disable — TOTP nulled + every active WebauthnCredential disabled. Body: { reason: string, notify_user: bool }. Fires MfaDisabled(trigger=admin_action). Audit-critical.

Regenerate + email new recovery codes to the User. Body: { reason: string }. Fires MfaRecoveryCodesRegenerated. Plaintext codes are NEVER returned in the HTTP response — they go by email only, to remove the operator's browser-history as an attack surface. Audit-warn.
